title: achillea distans subsp. alpina
intro: Achillea distans subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Yarrow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits associated with Alpine Yarrow include relief from menstrual cramps, reduction of fever, and treatment of digestive issues, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support women's health and alleviate various health conditions.
